content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward frameworks (ICM, RND) can be repurposed to solve the common problem of stagnant, repetitive code generation in task-specialized coding agents by rewarding exploration of unvisited syntactic patterns and underutilized API combinations, rather than only rewarding progress on the immediate user task. This creates self-improving coding agents that proactively discover new, more efficient coding solutions outside their training distribution without explicit human labeling of novel approaches.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
